title: I Put Linux in the Browser So You Don't Have to Install a VM
title_hi: I Put Linux in the Browser So You Don't Have to Install a VM
summary:
Hello everyone! I'm Asim Ayaz 

I'm a DevOps & InfoSec student and open-source contributor. I run Fedora as my daily
content:
Hello everyone! I'm Asim Ayaz 

I'm a DevOps & InfoSec student and open-source contributor. I run Fedora as my daily driver, and I love building free tools that lower the barrier to entry for people learning tech.

Today I want to share Web Linux a project I built because I was tired of telling my friends to "just install a VM" to practice Linux commands.


  
  
  The Problem


Every time someone asks me "how do I learn Linux?" I give them the same answer:


Download VirtualBox
Download an ISO
Allocate 4GB of RAM
Fight with display drivers for 3 hours
Finally open a terminal


By step 3, they've already given up.

I thought: "What if you could practice Linux commands directly in your browser? No install. No VM. Just a URL."


  
  
  The Solution: Web Linux


Web Linux is a browser-based Linux environment that lets you:


✅ Run common Linux commands in a terminal-like interface
✅ Practice file system navigation (ls, cd, pwd, mkdir)
✅ Learn command syntax without breaking your own system
✅ Access it from any device — phone, tablet, Chromebook
✅ Zero setup, zero install, zero risk


Live Demo: web-linux-delta.vercel.app


  
  
  Why This Matters


When I started learning Linux a year ago, I was terrified of typing the wrong command and bricking my system. Having a sandbox in the browser would've saved me weeks of anxiety.

Web Linux isn't a full VM, it's a safe, educational layer that behaves like a real terminal. Perfect for:


Students preparing for Linux certifications (LPIC, RHCSA, CompTIA Linux+)
Windows/Mac users curious about the command line
Beginners who want to practice rm -rf without consequences 😄



  
  
  The Tech Stack





Layer
Tech
Why




Frontend
HTML + CSS + Vanilla JS
Fast, no framework bloat


Terminal UI
Custom-built or Xterm.js
Familiar CLI feel in the browser


Logic
Client-side JavaScript
Instant feedback, no server latency


Hosting
Vercel
Free, fast, global edge network





  
  
  A Real Example


Type this in Web Linux:



$ ls -la
$ mkdir projects
$ cd projects
$ touch hello.txt
$ echo "Hello from Web Linux" &gt; hello.txt
$ cat hello.txt







  
  
  What I Learned



The browser is more powerful than we think
You don't always need a backend. Parsing commands, maintaining a virtual file system, and rendering output can all happen client-side.
UX matters for beginners
Real terminals are intimidating. A blinking cursor with no context is scary. I added:
Command hints and autocomplete suggestions
Color-coded output (errors in red, success in green)
A "cheat sheet" sidebar for common commands
Teaching is the best way to learn
Building this forced me to truly understand how Linux file systems work. I had to model cd, pwd, and relative paths in JavaScript. Now I know them cold.
What's Next
[ ] Pipe support (cat file.txt | grep "error")
[ ] More commands (chmod, chown, ps, top)
[ ] Interactive tutorials (e.g., "Learn grep in 5 minutes")
[ ] Dark mode toggle (because obviously)
Star the repo to follow along — I push updates every weekend.
Try It / Contribute
🔗 Live App: web-linux-delta.vercel.app
⭐ GitHub Repo: github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/web-linux
🐛 Found a bug or want a new command? Open an issue — I read every single one.
Did you learn Linux in a VM, WSL, or straight on bare metal? What's your advice for absolute beginners? Drop a comment below 👇

title: The Seller Said 2.3% Churn. The CSV Said 9.4%. Don't Make My $340K Mistake.
title_hi: The Seller Said 2.3% Churn. The CSV Said 9.4%. Don't Make My $340K Mistake.
summary:

The real number was 9.4%.

That gap cost me $340,000. And I
content:
I bought a SaaS business with 2.3% reported monthly churn.

The real number was 9.4%.

That gap cost me $340,000. And I didn't find it until four months after I wired the money.


  
  
  The Deal That Taught Me Everything


The CIM looked solid. The broker was reputable. The seller's numbers passed a surface-level sniff test. I did the standard diligence — P&L review, customer calls, cohort retention charts the seller provided.

What I didn't do: ask for the raw subscription CSV and compute the numbers myself.


  
  
  Where the Bodies Were Buried


When I finally extracted the raw billing data, here's what the seller's "2.3% churn" actually hid:



Logo vs. revenue churn divergence — losing 5% of logos representing 40% of MRR is a completely different business than 5% representing 5%

Customer concentration — 3 customers represented 60%+ of MRR. If any one churned post-close, my "SaaS" became a services firm overnight

Annual-plan decay — annual contracts all renewing in the same quarter with a 40% historical downgrade rate the seller "forgot" to mention

Zombie MRR — 12% of reported revenue came from customers who hadn't logged in for 90+ days. That revenue was already dead; it just hadn't been buried yet



  
  
  What I Built


ChurnLens — a buyer-side SaaS due-diligence tool that asks what the raw data actually shows, not what the seller reports.

Upload a subscription CSV and get a risk report covering: logo churn vs. revenue churn divergence, customer concentration vulnerability, annual-plan decay projection, zombie MRR detection, and an A-F revenue-quality grade.


  
  
  The Lesson


The seller hadn't committed fraud. They had made defensible methodology choices that painted a prettier picture. Logo churn on a count basis (2.3%) looked fine. Revenue churn on a dollar basis (9.4%) told a completely different story.

Never trust a summary. Always request the raw CSV.

Free Starter tier: 1 CSV analysis per month. No credit card.




Built by someone who paid $340,000 to learn this lesson so you don't have to.

title: I Was Tired of Scattered Classifieds Across 10 Different Sites — So I Built a Free Global Platform
title_hi: I Was Tired of Scattered Classifieds Across 10 Different Sites — So I Built a Free Global Platform
summary: Moving to a new city is exciting — until you realize how painful it is to find an apartment, a job, or even someone to f
content:
Moving to a new city is exciting — until you realize how painful it is to find an apartment, a job, or even someone to fix your sink.

Last year, I relocated to Toronto. I spent hours bouncing between Craigslist, Kijiji, Facebook Marketplace, Gumtree, and a dozen other niche boards. Some listings were outdated. Others were outright scams. And every platform had its own quirks, its own spam problems, its own "post and pray" culture.

I thought: why is finding basic life services still so fragmented in 2025?

That frustration became the seed for VEIGO — a free, global classifieds platform built to solve exactly this problem.


  
  
  The Problem I Kept Running Into


Let me paint the picture. When you move to a new city, you typically need four things fast:



A place to live — rental listings scattered across 5+ sites, with wildly inconsistent formats and photo quality

A job — job boards that either require expensive subscriptions or drown you in irrelevant listings

Gig work or services — finding a plumber, tutor, or delivery driver means asking around on WhatsApp groups or scrolling through sketchy Facebook pages

Tenders and contracts — if you're a freelancer or small business owner, finding local bidding opportunities is like searching for a needle in a haystack


Each of these needs lives on a different platform. None of them talk to each other. And if you're in a foreign country, the language barrier and unfamiliar platforms make it 10x worse.


  
  
  What I Built: VEIGO


VEIGO is a free global classifieds platform that brings all four of these categories under one roof:


  
  
  🏠 Rentals


Find apartments, rooms, and houses for rent. Whether you're looking in New York, Los Angeles, Toronto, Sydney, Vancouver, or London — we've got you covered with a clean, searchable interface that doesn't make you squint at tiny photos or deal with auto-playing videos.


  
  
  💼 Jobs


Post and browse job listings without the noise. VEIGO's job board is designed for clarity — clear titles, clear requirements, clear application process. No premium subscriptions needed to see contact information.


  
  
  🔧 Gig Work


Need a handyman? Want to offer tutoring? Looking for local delivery gigs? VEIGO's gig section connects service providers with people who need them, without the middleman fees that platforms like TaskRabbit charge.


  
  
  📋 Tenders & Bids


This is the one people overlook — but if you're a freelancer or small business, finding local tender opportunities is gold. VEIGO aggregates bidding and contract opportunities so you don't have to check 20 government and corporate procurement pages daily.


  
  
  Why "Free" Isn't a Marketing Gimmick


I get it — "free" usually means "we'll monetize your data" or "free for 30 days, then pay up."

VEIGO is different. The core classifieds platform is completely free to use. Post listings, browse listings, contact people — no hidden fees, no "premium" tiers that gate essential features.

We believe that access to basic classified information — housing, employment, services — should not be locked behind a paywall. Especially when you're in a new city and every dollar counts.


  
  
  The AI Tools That Make It Smarter


Beyond the classifieds themselves, VEIGO comes with a suite of AI-powered tools at tools.shopveigo.com that make the whole experience smoother:



Smart listing suggestions — describe what you're offering or looking for, and the AI helps you write better listings

Category optimization — the AI helps route your listing to the right category and city so it gets seen by the right people

Search assistance — natural language queries that actually understand what you mean, not just keyword matching


These tools aren't bolted on as an afterthought. They're core to the experience, because the whole point of VEIGO is to reduce friction in finding what you need.


  
  
  Why This Matters for Indie Hackers and Builders


If you're building something in the classifieds or local services space, you know the challenges:



Trust — how do you verify listings without killing the open nature of the platform?

Coverage — how do you get enough listings in enough cities to be useful?

Monetization — how do you keep the lights on without charging users?


These are problems we're actively working on, and I'd love to connect with other builders in this space. The classifieds market is worth billions globally, yet most platforms still look and feel like they were built in 2005.


  
  
  What's Next


We're currently live in New York, Los Angeles, Toronto, Sydney, Vancouver, and London, with more cities being added regularly. The platform is mobile-responsive and designed for the way people actually search — quickly, on their phones, while they're on the go.

If you've ever felt the pain of scattered classifieds, or if you're an indie hacker curious about building in this space, I'd love for you to check it out:

👉 VEIGO — Free Global Classifieds
👉 VEIGO AI Tools

Drop a comment if you have questions, feedback, or just want to chat about classifieds tech. I read every one.




Thanks for reading. If this resonated, share it with someone who's about to move to a new city — they'll thank you later.

title: HTTP QUERY Method: What It Is and Why It Matters
title_hi: HTTP QUERY Method: What It Is and Why It Matters
summary:
This post was created with AI assistance and reviewed for accuracy before publishing.

If you have been building REST AP
content:
This post was created with AI assistance and reviewed for accuracy before publishing.

If you have been building REST APIs for any length of time, you have hit the same awkward wall. You need to send a complex search query to the server, but GET requests cannot carry a body. So you either cram everything into query string parameters until the URL looks like a ransom note, or you misuse POST and pretend a read-only operation is a write. Neither feels right, because neither is right.

That frustration is exactly why the IETF is finalizing a new HTTP method: QUERY.


  
  
  What the QUERY Method Actually Does


QUERY is a safe, idempotent HTTP method designed to carry a request body for read-only retrieval operations. Think of it as GET with a body attached by design rather than by workaround.

The draft spec (draft-ietf-httpbis-safe-method-w-body) defines it clearly: a QUERY request retrieves a representation of a resource based on the content of the request body. The server is not expected to mutate state. The client is not expected to retry POST if the connection drops. Both sides understand the contract.

This matters because the safe and idempotent semantics are machine-readable promises. Caches can act on them. Load balancers can route around them. Middleware can log them differently. When you bend POST into a search endpoint, you lose all of that signaling.


  
  
  Why GET + Query Strings Breaks Down


Most developers discover the 2,048-character URL limit the hard way. You build a filter UI with 15 checkboxes, a date range picker, and a fulltext field. Users start combining options and suddenly some requests just fail. No error. The URL gets silently truncated in older proxies, or the server returns a 414.

The other footgun is encoding. Complex JSON filters inside a query string need to be percent-encoded, then decoded, then parsed. Every layer that touches the URL has a chance to mangle special characters. You end up writing defensive decode-and-retry logic that should not have to exist.

GraphQL took the most opinionated escape route: wrap everything in POST with a JSON body. It works. But it means you opt out of HTTP-level caching entirely unless you layer Persisted Queries or a caching proxy on top.

QUERY solves this at the protocol level instead of the application level.


  
  
  A Real Request Looks Like This





QUERY /products HTTP/1.1
Host: api.example.com
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "filters": {
    "category": "electronics",
    "price": { "min": 50, "max": 500 },
    "inStock": true
  },
  "sort": { "field": "price", "order": "asc" },
  "page": 1,
  "pageSize": 25
}






Compare that to the POST workaround. Functionally identical, but a POST tells every proxy, browser, and cache in the chain "this might write something." A QUERY says "this is a read, treat it accordingly."

On the server side with Node.js it is just another method to handle:



app.query('/products', async (req, res) => {
  const { filters, sort, page, pageSize } = req.body;
  const results = await db.products.search({ filters, sort, page, pageSize });
  res.json(results);
});






Express and most frameworks will need a router update to recognize the method name. Fetch on the client side already supports arbitrary method strings today:



const response = await fetch('/products', {
  method: 'QUERY',
  headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
  body: JSON.stringify(filters),
});






So you can start experimenting right now, even before widespread server-side router support lands.


  
  
  How It Compares to GET and POST


The table below cuts through the noise:




Concern
GET
POST
QUERY




Request body allowed
Technically, but ignored
Yes
Yes


Safe (no side effects)
Yes
No
Yes


Idempotent
Yes
No
Yes


Cacheable by default
Yes
No
Yes


Good for complex filters
No (URL limits)
Workaround
Yes




The cache story is where QUERY earns its keep. Because it is safe and idempotent, HTTP caches can store the response using the request body as part of the cache key. CDNs and reverse proxies can do the same once they implement the spec. You get the filtering power of a POST body with the caching behavior of GET.


  
  
  Where the Spec Stands Right Now


As of mid-2026 the draft is in late IETF review. It is not yet an RFC. That means you should not build production infrastructure that depends on universal QUERY support in every proxy and CDN in your stack. The method is not a standard yet.

What you can do is design your API surface with QUERY in mind so the migration is clean when support firms up. Route your complex search endpoints through a consistent pattern, document that the intent is safe-and-idempotent, and keep the request body structure tidy enough to eventually benefit from cache keying.

Watch the IETF tracker. When this lands as an RFC, every major framework will ship support within a release cycle. Being aware of it now means you are not scrambling to understand it later.


  
  
  What This Means for API Design


The arrival of QUERY is a quiet but meaningful correction to how HTTP maps to real-world operations. We have been living with the GET/POST mismatch for read-heavy APIs because there was no better option. Now there is one taking shape.

For new API designs I recommend sketching your read-with-filter endpoints with QUERY semantics even today. Use POST as a compatibility shim with a note in the comments. When framework support is stable, you swap the method and keep the body structure identical. The migration becomes a one-liner.

The HTTP spec does not evolve often. When it does, it usually reflects years of accumulated pain from real systems. QUERY is one of those moments. Pay attention.

title: How We Build Blazing-Fast Websites with Astro (and Why It Beats a JS-Heavy Stack)
title_hi: How We Build Blazing-Fast Websites with Astro (and Why It Beats a JS-Heavy Stack)
summary:
TL;DR


Astro ships zero JavaScript by default — pages render to static HTML, so they load fast without hydration overh
content:
TL;DR


Astro ships zero JavaScript by default — pages render to static HTML, so they load fast without hydration overhead.

Islands architecture lets you add interactivity only where you need it, hydrated independently, instead of shipping one giant JS bundle.
We lean on client:visible / client:idle directives, astro:assets image optimization, content collections, and view transitions to hit 95–100 Lighthouse scores.
Astro is framework-agnostic — bring React, Svelte, or Vue components as islands without rewriting them.
It's the right call for content-and-marketing-heavy sites where speed and SEO matter more than app-like complexity.



Every developer has shipped the site that looked great in dev and crawled in production. A marketing page — mostly text and images — somehow pulls 400KB of JavaScript, blocks the main thread, and posts a Largest Contentful Paint that makes your Core Web Vitals dashboard turn red. The content didn't need a framework runtime. The framework shipped one anyway.

That mismatch — static content paying the tax of a client-side app — is exactly the problem Astro was built to kill. Over the last couple of years it's become our default for performance-critical marketing sites, and the speed difference isn't marginal. So here's the practical version: how we build fast websites with Astro, the specific techniques that move the needle, and the honest cases where you shouldn't reach for it.


  
  
  Why Loading Speed Is Non-Negotiable in 2026


Speed stopped being a "nice to have" a while ago. Google's Core Web Vitals are a ranking factor, and Largest Contentful Paint, Interaction to Next Paint, and Cumulative Layout Shift all reward pages that render fast and stay stable. Slower than your competitors and you rank below them, full stop.

It's also a conversion issue. The correlation between load time and bounce rate is well documented — every extra second of load time bleeds users before they read a word. For a marketing site whose entire job is to convert visitors, performance is the product.

The frustrating part is that most slow sites are slow by accident. They're content pages built on tools designed for applications, so they ship an application's worth of JavaScript to render a paragraph. Astro's whole thesis is: stop doing that.


  
  
  What Actually Makes Astro Fast


Two ideas do most of the work.

Zero JavaScript by default. Astro renders your components to HTML at build time and ships no client-side JS unless you explicitly ask for it. A page of content becomes a page of HTML. The browser has almost nothing to parse, execute, or hydrate — it just paints. This alone eliminates the biggest cause of slow content sites.

Islands architecture. When you do need interactivity — a carousel, a search box, a menu — that component becomes an "island": a small, independently hydrated piece of interactivity in a sea of static HTML. Instead of hydrating the entire page, the browser hydrates only the islands, only when needed. You get interactivity where it matters and static speed everywhere else.

Put together: you ship the least JavaScript possible, and you ship it surgically. That's the foundation every technique below builds on.


  
  
  How We Build in Astro: The Techniques That Matter


Here's the actual playbook we run on a performance-first Astro build.


  
  
  1. HTML-first, JS-only-when-earned


We start every component as static. If it doesn't need to be interactive, it never becomes a client component. The default is HTML; interactivity is the exception you justify.


  
  
  2. Selective hydration with client directives


For the islands that do need JS, we hydrate them precisely:



client:idle for non-urgent widgets (hydrate when the browser is free)

client:visible for below-the-fold components (hydrate only when scrolled into view)

client:media for interactivity that's only relevant at certain breakpoints

client:load reserved for the rare above-the-fold interactive element


Most sites default everything to load-on-hydrate; deferring islands to visible/idle is one of the biggest wins for Interaction to Next Paint.


  
  
  3. Image optimization with astro:assets


Images are usually the real LCP culprit, not JS. Astro's built-in <Image /> component handles responsive sizing, modern formats (AVIF/WebP), and lazy loading automatically. We serve correctly sized, next-gen images without a manual pipeline — which quietly fixes most LCP problems.


  
  
  4. Content collections for content-driven sites


Blogs, case studies, and resource libraries live in type-safe content collections (Markdown/MDX with schema validation). Content stays fast, structured, and version-controlled — no CMS runtime tax on the front end.


  
  
  5. View transitions for app-like nav without the SPA weight


Astro's view transitions (ClientRouter) give smooth page-to-page transitions and can prefetch links, so navigation feels instant — without adopting a heavy client-side router or turning the whole site into a single-page app.


  
  
  6. Framework-agnostic islands


We're not locked into one UI library. Astro lets us drop in a React, Svelte, Solid, or Vue component as an island where it earns its place — reusing existing components without a rewrite, and still shipping zero JS everywhere else.


  
  
  7. Static-first deploy, SSR where needed


Most marketing pages are prerendered to static HTML and served from the edge (Vercel/Netlify/Cloudflare) for near-instant TTFB. For genuinely dynamic bits, Astro's server islands and SSR let us render just those on demand — without giving up static speed on the rest of the page.


  
  
  The Results (and How to Verify Them)


The payoff of this approach is consistent: content pages that render fast, ship minimal JavaScript, and post green Core Web Vitals. In practice we routinely land Lighthouse performance scores in the 95–100 range on Astro marketing builds, with sub-second Largest Contentful Paint on a decent connection.




e.g., "Our site: LCP 0.8s, Lighthouse Performance 99, total JS shipped: 18KB." Real numbers from your own build are far more credible than generic claims. Run it through PageSpeed Insights and quote the actual results.



  
  
  Astro vs the Usual Suspects (When It's the Right Call)




vs Next.js: Next is excellent for app-like, highly dynamic products. For content and marketing sites, Astro ships far less JS by default — you're not paying an app framework's overhead for a brochure site.

vs WordPress: WordPress carries plugin bloat and render-blocking scripts that tank performance. Astro's static output sidesteps that entire category of problem.

vs a no-code builder (Webflow/Framer): No-code wins when non-developers need to own and edit the site independently. Astro wins when you want maximum performance and full control and have engineers to maintain it. (In our shop we pick per project — Webflow when the client edits daily, Astro when raw speed is the priority.)



  
  
  When You Should Not Use Astro


Honesty matters. Astro is not the answer for a heavily interactive, app-like product (a dashboard, a real-time tool) — that's what a full client framework is for. It's also overkill if a non-technical team needs to build and edit everything themselves with no developer involved; a no-code platform serves them better. Astro shines for content-and-marketing-heavy sites where speed and SEO are the priority. Match the tool to the job.


  
  
  FAQ


Is Astro good for SEO?
Yes. Static, fast-loading HTML with strong Core Web Vitals is exactly what search engines reward, and Astro's output is clean and crawlable by default. Fast pages plus semantic HTML is the foundation of technical SEO.

Does Astro work with React or other frameworks?
Yes — Astro is framework-agnostic. You can use React, Svelte, Vue, or Solid components as hydrated islands, reusing existing components while keeping the rest of the page as zero-JS static HTML.

Can Astro handle dynamic content?
Yes. Beyond static generation, Astro supports SSR and server islands, so you can render dynamic pieces on demand while keeping the rest of the page static and fast.

Is Astro faster than Next.js?
For content and marketing sites, typically yes — because Astro ships zero JS by default while Next hydrates a client app. For highly interactive products, Next's model may fit better. Match the framework to the workload.


  
  
  The Bottom Line


Fast websites aren't about a magic framework — they're about not shipping what you don't need. Astro makes that the default: static HTML first, JavaScript only where it earns its place, images optimized automatically, interactivity added as isolated islands. For content and marketing sites where speed, SEO, and conversion are the goals, it's the cleanest path we've found to a site that loads in a blink and stays green on Core Web Vitals.




Written by the team at *Windmark** — a strategy-first design & development studio. We build fast, high-converting websites the right way for each project: Webflow when your team needs to own and edit it, Astro when raw performance is the priority. See our work at thewindmark.com or start a conversation.*

title: I Fire 50 Research Prompts Before Bed and Read the Answers with Coffee
title_hi: मैं रात को 50 रिसर्च प्रॉम्प्ट्स भेजता हूं और कॉफी के साथ जवाब पढ़ता हूं
summary: Research with Gemini's background tier for free
content: Something slipped into the Gemini API that I don't think enough people noticed: you can run a prompt in the background. You send it, get an interaction ID back in under a second, and the model keeps working server-side while you close your laptop. No held-open connection, no keeping a tab alive. I built a client called gemcatch to manage these IDs and prompts, making it easy to research topics without babysitting a terminal. With gemcatch, I can submit tasks like 'compare the 2026 EU AI Act timeline against the UK approach' and retrieve the results whenever I'm ready.
content_hi: जेमिनी एपीआई में कुछ ऐसा है जो मुझे लगता है कि बहुत से लोगों ने नहीं देखा: आप प्रॉम्प्ट को पृष्ठभूमि में चला सकते हैं। आप इसे भेजते हैं, एक सेकंड से कम समय में इंटरैक्शन आईडी वापस प्राप्त करते हैं, और मॉडल सर्वर-साइड काम करता रहता है जब आप अपना लैपटॉप बंद कर देते हैं। मैंने एक क्लाइंट बनाया है जिसे gemcatch कहते हैं, जो इन आईडी और प्रॉम्प्ट्स को प्रबंधित करना आसान बनाता है।

title: Maximize Auto Shop Efficiency: Reduce No-Shows
title_hi: अपनी ऑटो दुकान में नो-शोज़ को कम करें
summary: Reduce auto shop no-shows with automation
content: An empty bay can be costly for your auto shop. To reduce no-shows, transition from manual reminders to automated website confirmations and reminders. Most missed appointments are caused by a flawed booking process, not irresponsible customers. A single missed appointment can cost around $220 to $295, considering the idle bay, technician, and potential alternative service.
content_hi: एक खाली बे आपकी ऑटो दुकान के लिए महंगा हो सकता है। नो-शोज़ को कम करने के लिए, मैनुअल रिमाइंडर से स्वचालित वेबसाइट पुष्टिकरण और रिमाइंडर में स्थानांतरित करें। अधिकांश मिस्ड अपॉइंटमेंट्स असफल बुकिंग प्रक्रिया के कारण होते हैं, गैर-जिम्मेदार ग्राहकों के नहीं। एक मिस्ड अपॉइंटमेंट की लागत लगभग $220 से $295 तक हो सकती है, खाली बे, तकनीशियन और संभावित वैकल्पिक सेवा को ध्यान में रखते हुए।

title: I Automated My Developer Portfolio (and You Can Too)
title_hi: मैंने अपने डेवलपर पोर्टफोलियो को स्वचालित किया (और आप भी कर सकते हैं)
summary: Automate your developer portfolio with DevCard AI
content: Every developer knows they should have a portfolio, but nobody enjoys building one. So I built DevCard AI — point it at your GitHub profile or paste a resume, and it writes a styled, interactive portfolio for you. What makes it different from a template is the bio written by an AI in a voice you pick, and six unique themes to choose from. The free tier gives 3 generations, and one-time run packs are available for $5 for 10 runs.
content_hi: प्रत्येक डेवलपर को पता है कि उनके पास एक पोर्टफोलियो होना चाहिए, लेकिन कोई भी इसे बनाने का आनंद नहीं लेता। इसलिए मैंने डेवकार्ड एआई बनाया — अपने गिटहब प्रोफाइल पर इसे निर्देशित करें या एक रिज्यूमे पेस्ट करें, और यह आपके लिए एक स्टाइलिश, इंटरैक्टिव पोर्टफोलियो लिखता है।

title: Building Production-Grade LLM Evaluation Pipelines
title_hi: उत्पादन-ग्रेड एलएलएम मूल्यांकन पाइपलाइन्स का निर्माण: वाइब्स से मीट्रिक्स तक
summary: Automated LLM evaluation pipeline for production-grade results
content: We replaced 'looks good to me' with automated evaluation, catching 92% of hallucinations before deployment. Our team shipped a RAG-based customer support assistant, but it failed in production, providing hallucinated responses to customers. We learned that 'vibe checks' are not enough and that production evaluation needs domain-specific judges, speed, regression detection, CI/CD integration, and golden dataset management.
content_hi: हमने 'यह दिखने में सही लगता है' को स्वचालित मूल्यांकन से बदल दिया, जिससे तैनाती से पहले 92% हॉलुसिनेशन पकड़े गए। हमारी टीम ने एक आरएजी-आधारित ग्राहक सहायता सहायक को जहाज किया, लेकिन यह उत्पादन में विफल रहा, ग्राहकों को हॉलुसिनेटेड प्रतिक्रियाएं प्रदान की। हमने सीखा कि 'वाइब चेक' पर्याप्त नहीं हैं और उत्पादन मूल्यांकन के लिए डोमेन-विशिष्ट निर्णायक, गति, प्रतिगमन पहचान, सीआई/सीडी एकीकरण और स्वर्ण डेटासेट प्रबंधन की आवश्यकता है।

title: Optimizing RAG at Scale: Boosting Efficiency
title_hi: आरएजी को बड़े पैमाने पर अनुकूलित करना
summary: RAG optimization techniques for improved latency
content: We've reimagined our RAG retrieval pipeline, shifting from a 'semantic search + hope' approach to a measured, tunable system. This change has significantly enhanced our efficiency, with a notable 40% reduction in latency and achieving 95% recall@10. The previous method involved chunking by 512 tokens, using text-embedding-3-small, and top-k=5, which, although sufficient for demos, faced challenges in production. Issues arose with legal contracts being split mid-sentence, API docs being overwhelmed by noise, and customer tickets requiring conversational context overlap. Our new approach focuses on rebuilding the retrieval layer from the ground up, emphasizing chunking strategies that cater to diverse content types.
content_hi: हमने अपनी आरएजी पुनर्प्राप्ति पाइपलाइन को पुनः कल्पना की है, 'सेमेंटिक खोज + आशा' दृष्टिकोण से एक मापनीय, ट्यून करने योग्य प्रणाली में स्थानांतरित किया है। इससे हमारी कार्यक्षमता में काफी सुधार हुआ है, जिसमें 40% की विलंबता में कमी और 95% रिकॉल@10 हासिल किया गया है। पिछली विधि में 512 टोकन द्वारा छोटे करना, टेक्स्ट-एम्बेडिंग-3-स्मॉल का उपयोग करना, और टॉप-के=5 शामिल था, जो डेमो के लिए पर्याप्त था, लेकिन उत्पादन में चुनौतियों का सामना किया।

title: 007: First Light's Original Vision
title_hi: ००७: फर्स्ट लाइट का मूल दृष्टिकोण
summary: 007: First Light's original concept
content: The latest revelation about 007: First Light has shed new light on the game's original concept. Initially, the game was not meant to focus solely on James Bond. Instead, it was envisioned as an ensemble piece, where all the 00 agents would work together, much like they do in high-stakes missions. You had the different agents working together, each bringing their unique skills to the table. This approach would have added a fresh twist to the traditional Bond formula, allowing players to experience the world of espionage through multiple perspectives.

title: GTA 6 PC Delay: A Blessing in Disguise
title_hi: जीटीए 6 पीसी पर नहीं आएगा लॉन्च पर: यह एक अच्छी बात है
summary: GTA 6 PC delay explained
content: The upcoming GTA 6 has been making waves in the gaming community, but PC gamers might be disappointed to know that it won't be available on their platform at launch. There are three main reasons for this delay. Firstly, the game's developer, Rockstar Games, wants to focus on optimizing the game for consoles. Secondly, the company is concerned about the potential for piracy on PC. Lastly, Rockstar wants to avoid the backlash it faced with the PC launch of GTA 5, which was plagued by bugs and performance issues. Despite the initial disappointment, this delay might be a good thing for PC gamers in the long run.
content_hi: आगामी जीटीए 6 ने गेमिंग समुदाय में हलचल मचा दी है, लेकिन पीसी गेमर्स को यह जानकर निराशा हो सकती है कि यह लॉन्च पर उनके प्लेटफॉर्म पर उपलब्ध नहीं होगा। इसके तीन मुख्य कारण हैं। पहला, गेम के डेवलपर रॉकस्टार गेम्स चाहते हैं कि वे कंसोल के लिए गेम को अनुकूलित करने पर ध्यान केंद्रित करें। दूसरा, कंपनी पीसी पर संभावित पायरेसी के बारे में चिंतित है। तीसरा, रॉकस्टार जीटीए 5 के पीसी लॉन्च के साथ जो प्रतिक्रिया हुई थी, उसे避ाना चाहता है, जिसमें बग और प्रदर्शन समस्याएं थीं।

summary: Kubernetes storage made easy

title: I Built 31 Developer Tools in a Weekend
title_hi: मैंने एक सप्ताहांत में 31 डेवलपर टूल्स बनाए - यहाँ क्या सीखा
summary: DevKits: 31 browser-based developer tools for JSON, security, and more
content: As a developer, I often find myself jumping between various websites to perform tasks like formatting JSON, decoding JWT, or converting Unix timestamps. However, these sites are often cluttered with ads, popups, and banners, compromising privacy. To address this issue, I created DevKits, a single site with 31 developer tools that run entirely in the browser, ensuring no uploads, accounts, or tracking. DevKits offers a range of tools across categories like JSON, converters, encoding, security, and more. You can access it at devkits.vip.
content_hi: एक डेवलपर के रूप में, मैं अक्सर विभिन्न वेबसाइटों के बीच कूदता हूं ताकि मैं JSON फॉर्मेट कर सकूं, JWT डीकोड कर सकूं, या यूनिक्स टाइमस्टाम्प कनवर्ट कर सकूं। हालांकि, ये साइटें अक्सर विज्ञापनों, पॉप-अप और बैनरों से भरी होती हैं, जो गोपनीयता को खतरे में डालती हैं। इस समस्या को हल करने के लिए, मैंने डेवकिट्स बनाया, एक एकल साइट जिसमें 31 डेवलपर टूल्स हैं जो पूरी तरह से ब्राउज़र में चलते हैं, जिससे कोई अपलोड, खाते या ट्रैकिंग नहीं होती है।

title: Build Authentication in Next.js 15 From Scratch
title_hi: नेक्स्ट.जेएस 15 में प्रमाणीकरण बनाएं - स्क्रैच से
summary: Next.js 15 authentication from scratch
content: When it comes to authentication in Next.js, many developers immediately reach for NextAuth. However, to truly understand how authentication works, it's beneficial to build it yourself from scratch. This approach allows for a deeper understanding of the underlying mechanics. In this guide, we'll explore how to implement complete JWT authentication in Next.js 15 using only the jsonwebtoken and bcryptjs libraries. The process includes user registration with hashed passwords, login functionality that utilizes JWT tokens, protected API routes, middleware for page protection, and token refresh capabilities.
content_hi: नेक्स्ट.जेएस में प्रमाणीकरण की बात आती है, तो अधिकांश डेवलपर्स तुरंत नेक्स्टऑथ का उपयोग करने लगते हैं। हालांकि, प्रमाणीकरण के काम करने के तरीके को वास्तव में समझने के लिए, यह फायदेमंद है कि आप इसे स्वयं स्क्रैच से बनाएं। यह दृष्टिकोण अंतर्निहित यांत्रिकी की गहरी समझ प्रदान करता है। इस मार्गदर्शिका में, हम नेक्स्ट.जेएस 15 में jsonwebtoken और bcryptjs लाइब्रेरी का उपयोग करके पूर्ण JWT प्रमाणीकरण कैसे लागू करें, इस पर जानकारी प्रदान करेंगे।

title: Streamlining Medical Writing with AI: Ensuring Regulatory Accuracy
title_hi: एआई संचालित मेडिकल लेखन: नियामक सटीकता सुनिश्चित करना
summary: Boost medical writing efficiency with AI-powered validation
content:
As a solo medical writer, ensuring regulatory accuracy can be a daunting task. The Three-Layer Validation Framework provides a robust solution, with Layer 1 automated checks catching 80% of errors. Utilizing tools like ConsistencyCheck AI, which runs automated consistency checks, can significantly reduce extra hours spent on validation. 
   
   The core principle of layered validation enables medical writers to focus on high-level tasks. For instance, when writing about clinical trials, ConsistencyCheck AI can help ensure that all trial protocols are consistently formatted. Implementation involves high-level steps such as integrating AI-powered tools, training on the Three-Layer framework, and regularly reviewing validation protocols.
   
   In conclusion, the key takeaway is that AI-powered validation protocols, such as the Three-Layer framework, can ensure regulatory accuracy without requiring extra hours from solo medical writers. By leveraging tools like ConsistencyCheck AI, medical writers can streamline their workflow and focus on producing high-quality content.
content_hi:
एकल मेडिकल लेखक के रूप में, नियामक सटीकता सुनिश्चित करना एक चुनौतीपूर्ण काम हो सकता है। तीन-परत वाले मान्यकरण ढांचे में पहली परत के स्वचालित जांच 80% त्रुटियों को पकड़ लेते हैं। संगति जांच एआई जैसे उपकरणों का उपयोग करके, जो स्वचालित संगति जांच चलाता है, मान्यकरण पर अतिरिक्त घंटे बिताने को काफी कम किया जा सकता है। 
   
   स्तरीकृत मान्यकरण का मूल सिद्धांत मेडिकल लेखकों को उच्च-स्तरीय कार्यों पर ध्यान केंद्रित करने में सक्षम बनाता है। उदाहरण के लिए, जब नैदानिक परीक्षणों के बारे में लिखते समय, संगति जांच एआई यह सुनिश्चित करने में मदद कर सकता है कि सभी परीक्षण प्रोटोकॉल लगातार प्रारूप में हैं। कार्यान्वयन में उच्च-स्तरीय चरण शामिल हैं जैसे कि एआई-संचालित उपकरणों को एकीकृत करना, तीन-परत ढांचे पर प्रशिक्षण देना, और नियमित रूप से मान्यकरण प्रोटोकॉल की समीक्षा करना।
   
   निष्कर्ष में, मुख्य बात यह है कि एआई-संचालित मान्यकरण प्रोटोकॉल, जैसे कि तीन-परत ढांचा, एकल मेडिकल लेखकों के लिए नियामक सटीकता सुनिश्चित कर सकते हैं बिना अतिरिक्त घंटे की आवश्यकता के। संगति जांच एआई जैसे उपकरणों का लाभ उठाकर, मेडिकल लेखक अपने कार्य प्रवाह को सुव्यवस्थित कर सकते हैं और उच्च गुणवत्ता वाली सामग्री का उत्पादन करने पर ध्यान केंद्रित कर सकते हैं।

title: RAG for Beginners: Unlocking Document Q&A with LangChain
title_hi: आरएजी के लिए एक परिचय: लैंगचेन के साथ दस्तावेज़ प्रश्नोत्तर प्रणाली बनाएं
summary: Build a document Q&A system with RAG and LangChain
content: Large Language Models (LLMs) have limited knowledge of private documents. RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) solves this by combining document retrieval with LLMs. This post introduces RAG and provides a full working code example using LangChain. The process involves two steps: retrieval and generation. With LangChain, you can build a document Q&A system that leverages your private documents to answer questions accurately.
content_hi: बड़े भाषा मॉडल (एलएलएम) को निजी दस्तावेजों की सीमित जानकारी होती है। आरएजी (पुनर्प्राप्ति-सहयोजित पीढ़ी) इस समस्या का समाधान करता है जो दस्तावेज़ पुनर्प्राप्ति और एलएलएम को जोड़ता है। इस पोस्ट में आरएजी और लैंगचेन का उपयोग करके एक पूरी कार्यशील कोड उदाहरण पेश किया गया है। प्रक्रिया में दो चरण शामिल हैं: पुनर्प्राप्ति और पीढ़ी। लैंगचेन के साथ, आप एक दस्तावेज़ प्रश्नोत्तर प्रणाली बना सकते हैं जो सटीक उत्तर देने के लिए अपने निजी दस्तावेजों का लाभ उठाती है।

title: AI Memory: A Hierarchical Approach
title_hi: एआई मेमोरी: एक संरचित दृष्टिकोण
summary: AI memory with hierarchical docs
content: Effective project memory for AI agents isn't about accumulating documents, but rather organizing them in a hierarchical table of contents. This structure enables agents to quickly locate relevant documents and disregard irrelevant ones. By utilizing directories, filenames, and README.md files, agents can navigate through the project documentation efficiently. A well-structured documentation system allows agents to find specific information, such as specs, architecture, and tests, without having to read the entire project. Good project memory does not expose everything all the time, instead, it uses paths and indexes to help agents find what they need and exclude unnecessary documents.
content_hi: एआई एजेंटों के लिए प्रभावी प्रोजेक्ट मेमोरी दस्तावेजों के ढेर को बढ़ाने के बारे में नहीं है, बल्कि इसे एक संरचित सामग्री सूची में व्यवस्थित करने के बारे में है। यह संरचना एजेंटों को तेजी से प्रासंगिक दस्तावेजों का पता लगाने और अप्रासंगिक लोगों को अस्वीकार करने में सक्षम बनाती है।

summary: Cline AI coding assistant breach exposed
content: A recent GitHub issue title exploit compromised Cline, an AI coding assistant used by over 5 million developers. The issue arose when a misconfigured trigger condition granted any GitHub user the ability to invoke a workflow, allowing an AI agent to run shell commands. An injected instruction flooded the CI cache with junk data, evicting legitimate entries and paving the way for further exploitation.

title: The Underpowered Test Deception
title_hi: कम शक्ति वाले परीक्षण का झूठ
summary: Underpowered tests can lead to incorrect results
content: While auditing statistics in my ad-testing agent, Mia, I discovered a 20x error that had gone undetected. The issue stemmed from a shortcut formula that estimated 1,111 users per arm of an A/B test, whereas the actual formula required 22,278 users at a 1.9% click-through rate. This miscalculation led to underpowered tests that still reported winners, despite lacking sufficient data. I've encountered similar failures in AI systems across various industries, where the absence of errors masked incorrect answers.
content_hi: मैंने अपने विज्ञापन-परीक्षण एजेंट, मिया में आंकड़ों की जांच की और एक 20 गुना त्रुटि पाई जो पहले कभी नहीं पकड़ी गई थी। समस्या एक शॉर्टकट सूत्र से उत्पन्न हुई जिसने एक ए/बी परीक्षण के प्रत्येक भाग में 1,111 उपयोगकर्ताओं का अनुमान लगाया, जबकि वास्तविक सूत्र ने 1.9% क्लिक-थ्रू दर पर 22,278 उपयोगकर्ताओं की आवश्यकता थी।

content: Le Chat is transforming backlink prospect research by generating qualified targets in minutes using natural-language prompts. This AI-powered tool replaces manual spreadsheet work with structured prompts, returning filtered prospect lists for outreach. By leveraging Le Chat's real-time web access, users gain an edge over static-training models. Pairing Le Chat with a platform like SEOintent streamlines the process, scaling it across hundreds of keywords.
content_hi: ले चैट बैकलिंक प्रोस्पेक्ट रिसर्च को बदल रहा है जो प्राकृतिक-भाषा प्रेरितों का उपयोग करके योग्य लक्ष्यों को मिनटों में उत्पन्न करता है। यह एआई-संचालित उपकरण मैनुअल स्प्रेडशीट कार्य को संरचित प्रेरितों से बदल देता है, जो आउट्रीच के लिए फ़िल्टर्ड प्रोस्पेक्ट सूची लौटाता है।

title: Waymo Resumes San Francisco Service After Brief Pause
title_hi: वेमो की सैन फ्रांसिस्को सेवा एक घंटे के पause के बाद फिर से शुरू
summary: Waymo SF service resumes after pause
content: Waymo's self-driving taxi service in San Francisco experienced a brief pause due to a power outage, but it has now resumed operations. This isn't the first time power outages have caused issues for Waymo, highlighting the need for reliable infrastructure to support autonomous vehicles. The company has been working to improve its services and expand its reach in the city.
content_hi: वेमो की स्व-ड्राइविंग टैक्सी सेवा सैन फ्रांसिस्को में बिजली की आपूर्ति में रुकावट के कारण थोड़े समय के लिए रुक गई, लेकिन अब यह फिर से शुरू हो गई है। यह वेमो के लिए पहली बार नहीं है जब बिजली की आपूर्ति में रुकावट ने समस्याएं पैदा की हैं, जो स्वचालित वाहनों को समर्थन देने के लिए विश्वसनीय बुनियादी ढांचे की आवश्यकता को दर्शाता है।

title: Christopher Nolan's Next Film Delayed: A 3-Year Wait
title_hi: क्रिस्टोफर नोलन की अगली फिल्म में तीन साल की देरी
summary: Christopher Nolan's next film delayed until 2026
content: Acclaimed director Christopher Nolan has revealed that his next film won't be released for at least three years. This delay comes after the grueling experience of directing The Odyssey, which pushed him to his limits. Nolan is known for his complex and visually stunning films, and it's clear that he's taking the time he needs to ensure his next project meets his high standards. Fans will have to wait patiently for his next cinematic masterpiece.
content_hi: प्रसिद्ध निर्देशक क्रिस्टोफर नोलन ने खुलासा किया है कि उनकी अगली फिल्म कम से कम तीन साल बाद रिलीज होगी। यह देरी ओडिसी का निर्देशन करने के बाद आई है, जिसने उन्हें अपनी सीमाओं तक धकेल दिया। नोलन को अपनी जटिल और दृष्टिभ्रम फिल्मों के लिए जाना जाता है, और यह स्पष्ट है कि वह अपने अगले परियोजना के लिए समय ले रहे हैं।

title: Call of Duty Movie Set in Modern Warfare Universe
title_hi: कॉल ऑफ ड्यूटी मूवी मॉडर्न वॉरफेयर ब्रह्मांड में सेट है
summary: Call of Duty movie in Modern Warfare universe
content: Paramount Pictures and Activision have confirmed that the upcoming Call of Duty movie will be set in the Modern Warfare universe. This move is likely to excite fans of the popular video game series, as Modern Warfare is one of its most successful and beloved storylines. The movie's director has also confirmed this news, sparking speculation about the plot and characters that will be featured.
content_hi: पैरामाउंट पिक्चर्स और एक्टिविज़न ने पुष्टि की है कि आगामी कॉल ऑफ ड्यूटी मूवी मॉडर्न वॉरफेयर ब्रह्मांड में सेट होगी। यह कदम शायद लोकप्रिय वीडियो गेम श्रृंखला के प्रशंसकों को उत्साहित करेगा, क्योंकि मॉडर्न वॉरफेयर इसकी सबसे सफल और प्यारी कहानियों में से एक है। मूवी के निर्देशक ने भी इस खबर की पुष्टि की है, जिससे प्लॉट और पात्रों के बारे में कयास लगाए जा रहे हैं।
